vulnerabilities.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service in remarkable
vulnerabilities.description: lib/common/html_re.js in remarkable 1.7.1 allows Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via a CDATA section.

vulnerabilities.title: XSS in Data URI in remarkable
vulnerabilities.description:
Affected versions of `remarkable` are vulnerable to cross-site scripting. Vulnerable versions of the package allow the use of `data:` URIs in links, and can therefore execute javascript. 


## Proof of Concept

```markdown
[link](data:text/html,<script>alert('0')</script>)
```


## Recommendation

Update to v1.7.0 or later

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-site Scripting in remarkable
vulnerabilities.description: In remarkable 1.7.1, lib/parser_inline.js mishandles URL filtering, which allows attackers to trigger XSS via unprintable characters, as demonstrated by a `\x0ejavascript:` URL.

vulnerabilities.title: Content Injection in remarkable
vulnerabilities.description: Versions 1.4.0 and earlier of `remarkable` are affected by a cross-site scripting vulnerability. This occurs because vulnerable versions of `remarkable` did not properly whitelist link protocols, and consequently allowed `javascript:` to be used. ### Proof of Concept Markdown Source: ``` [link]() ``` Rendered HTML: ``` link ``` ## Recommendation Update to version 1.4.1 or later
